name: AI-enhanced Educational Game Design — Cohort 2026-Spring

issuer.name: The University of Alabama — College of Education

achievement.name: AI-enhanced Educational Game Design
achievement.description: Proficient on 25 non-compensatory rubric criteria across D1–D5.

criteria.narrative: Proficient on every criterion of every deliverable (D1–D5).

result.resultDescription: D1 · Design Problem Statement — 5/5 criteria at Proficient

evidence.name: D1 · Design Problem Statement
evidence.description: Proficient on all 5 criteria. Includes tracked-changes revision based on peer feedback.

evidence.name: D2 · Objective × Mechanic Crosswalk
evidence.description: 3 objectives + 1 declined row. Every row cites a D1 context constraint.

evidence.name: D3 · Paper Prototype + Facilitator Guide
evidence.description: A non-author ran the full loop from the guide in 4 minutes. Iteration log covers 4 cycles including one reverted change.

evidence.name: D4 · Playtest Report
evidence.description: Four target-learner sessions. Observation-vs-interpretation separation present.

evidence.name: D5 · Implementation Spec
evidence.description: State machine, event map, Three.js bridge for one hero scene, and Known Limits annex drawn from the S11 backlog.

_proof_note: The proof block above is a *placeholder*. A real issuance pipeline signs the canonicalized credential with an Ed25519 key bound to the issuer DID; verifiers reconstruct the canonical form and check the signature. See https://www.w3.org/TR/vc-data-integrity/ and https://w3c-ccg.github.io/di-eddsa-2020/. For this reference handbook we publish the unsigned example and document the signing pipeline separately.
